name: Godot AI MCP Plugin
what_it_does: Godot AI MCP plugin enabling AI agents to interact with the Godot game engine — reading scene trees, manipulating nodes and properties, creating and editing GDScript code, running game simulations, accessing engine APIs, and integrating AI-driven assistance into Godot game development workflows for automated scene creation and code generation.
use_cases: Generating GDScript code and scenes from game development agents
Reading and modifying Godot scene tree from procedural generation agents
Automating repetitive game development tasks from productivity agents
Debugging game logic by inspecting scene state from debugging agents
Creating game assets and level layouts from creative AI agents
Integrating AI NPCs and behavior trees from game AI agents
not_for: Teams using Unity, Unreal, or other game engines (Godot-specific)
Runtime game AI (this is editor/development integration, not in-game AI)
Production deployments (editor plugin intended for development workflows)
best_when: A Godot game developer wants AI assistance with scene creation, GDScript coding, or game automation — the plugin bridges the Godot editor with AI agents for development productivity.
avoid_when: You're using Unity, Unreal, or other game engines, or need runtime in-game AI capabilities rather than editor assistance.

auth.notes: No authentication — Godot editor plugin communicates locally with MCP server via stdio. Godot engine must be installed and project open in editor.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Godot is free and open source (MIT); plugin is free
pricing.notes: Godot engine is free and open source (MIT license). Plugin is free. No external service costs.

performance.rate_limits: Local Godot editor — no external rate limits

known_agent_gotchas: Godot editor must be open with project loaded — MCP cannot start Godot automatically
Plugin must be installed in Godot project via addon system — setup required per project
GDScript code generation quality depends on agent's knowledge of Godot 4.x API changes
Scene manipulation affects live editor state — save frequently to avoid losing work
Godot 4.x vs 3.x API differences — ensure MCP supports your Godot version
Niche community plugin — expect limited documentation and possible stability issues
agent_readiness.security_notes: Local Godot editor process. No external API calls. No credentials required. Game project files modified directly — use version control (git) for safety.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local process. No external calls. No credentials. Use git for project safety. Community plugin.
